# Speedcube

> Online Rubik's Cube solver, speedcubing timer, notation guides, and algorithm library.

## Main Pages

- [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/solve/): Solve supported puzzle scrambles and play solution animations.
- [Timer](https://speedcube.com.br/timer/): Practice speedcubing sessions with scrambles, inspection, and averages.
- [Algorithms](https://speedcube.com.br/algorithms/): Browse algorithm sets by puzzle and method.
- [Notation Guides](https://speedcube.com.br/notations/3x3/): Learn move notation for supported puzzles.

## Language Routes

- English (United States): unprefixed routes, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/solve/).
- Portuguese (Brazil): `/pt-BR`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/pt-BR/solve/).
- Spanish: `/es`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/es/solve).
- Italian: `/it`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/it/solve).
- German: `/de`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/de/solve).
- French: `/fr`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/fr/solve).
- Russian: `/ru`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/ru/solve).
- Simplified Chinese: `/zh`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/zh/solve).
- Japanese: `/ja`, for example [Solver](https://speedcube.com.br/ja/solve).

## Notes For Agents

- Prefer canonical URLs on `https://speedcube.com.br`.
- English (`en-US`) is canonical on unprefixed routes; `/en/` is legacy and redirects to the unprefixed URL.
- Every other supported language uses an explicit prefix with the same URL slugs.
- Explicit URL prefixes determine language. `/` permanently redirects to the unprefixed English solver.
- Solver correctness belongs to the Rust API and cube engine.
